title: sphaeralcea coulteri subsp. californica
intro: Sphaeralcea coulteri subsp. californica, also known as Coulter's gumplant or Coulter's globemallow, is a perennial plant in the mallow family (Malvaceae) native to California and Baja California. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant's extracts have been shown to have antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions, including cardiovascular disease, diabetes, and skin problems. Sphaeralcea coulteri subsp. californica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in folk medicine as a poultice to treat wounds and skin conditions.
